mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures contemporaneous consumer spending strength, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive RSAFS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or rank correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.5% restricts credit availability and raises discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate crosses above this threshold.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, or the mean return in triggered periods is not statistically lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and falling industrial production identifies a simultaneous labor-market and supply-contraction shock that amplifies recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint z-score of UNRATE and INDPRO does not produce a rank correlation below -0.05 with 60-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term is insignificant (p > 0.05) in a regression controlling for main effects.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns only during post-recession recoveries when capacity utilization is low and rebound potential is high; outside this regime the signal is absent.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession periods, positive INDPRO growth does not coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of observations, or the Sharpe ratio of the conditional signal is below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: Higher-than-expected inflation captured by month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases erodes real corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns following trigger events are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of construction employment and durable goods demand; sustained high levels of HOUST predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between HOUST and 60-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or average 60-day returns in the top quartile of HOUST are not higher than in the bottom quartile (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and imminent consumption slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns following a 0.2pp UNRATE increase are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the trigger fails to produce negative returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding household income and aggregate demand, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between month-over-month PAYEMS change and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to coincide with positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations.
